issue.message: Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES javadoc for details.
issue.summary: Target SDK attribute is not targeting latest version
issue.explanation:
When your application runs on a version of Android that is more recent than your `targetSdkVersion` specifies that it has been tested with, various compatibility modes kick in. This ensures that your application continues to work, but it may look out of place. For example, if the `targetSdkVersion` is less than 14, your app may get an option button in the UI.
To fix this issue, set the `targetSdkVersion` to the highest available value. Then test your app to make sure everything works correctly. You may want to consult the compatibility notes to see what changes apply to each version you are adding support for: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Build.VERSION_CODES.html as well as follow this guide: https://developer.android.com/distribute/best-practices/develop/target-sdk.html

issue.message: Avoid passing `null` as the view root (needed to resolve layout parameters on the inflated layout's root element)
issue.explanation: When inflating a layout, avoid passing in null as the parent view, since otherwise any layout parameters on the root of the inflated layout will be ignored.

issue.message: Redundant label can be removed
issue.explanation: When an activity does not have a label attribute, it will use the one from the application tag. Since the application has already specified the same label, the label on this activity can be omitted.

issue.message: Redundant label can be removed
issue.explanation: When an activity does not have a label attribute, it will use the one from the application tag. Since the application has already specified the same label, the label on this activity can be omitted.

issue.message: A newer version of androidx.appcompat:appcompat than 1.6.1 is available: 1.7.1
issue.explanation: This detector looks for usages of libraries where the version you are using is not the current stable release. Using older versions is fine, and there are cases where you deliberately want to stick with an older version. However, you may simply not be aware that a more recent version is available, and that is what this lint check helps find.

issue.message: A newer version of com.google.android.material:material than 1.11.0 is available: 1.13.0
issue.explanation: This detector looks for usages of libraries where the version you are using is not the current stable release. Using older versions is fine, and there are cases where you deliberately want to stick with an older version. However, you may simply not be aware that a more recent version is available, and that is what this lint check helps find.

issue.message: Use databinding or explicit wiring of click listener in code
issue.summary: Using `android:onClick` on older version of the platform is broken
issue.explanation: Old versions of the platform do not properly support resolving `android:onClick`

issue.message: Use databinding or explicit wiring of click listener in code
issue.summary: Using `android:onClick` on older version of the platform is broken
issue.explanation: Old versions of the platform do not properly support resolving `android:onClick`

issue.message: Use databinding or explicit wiring of click listener in code
issue.summary: Using `android:onClick` on older version of the platform is broken
issue.explanation: Old versions of the platform do not properly support resolving `android:onClick`

issue.message: Use databinding or explicit wiring of click listener in code
issue.summary: Using `android:onClick` on older version of the platform is broken
issue.explanation: Old versions of the platform do not properly support resolving `android:onClick`

issue.message: Use databinding or explicit wiring of click listener in code
issue.summary: Using `android:onClick` on older version of the platform is broken
issue.explanation: Old versions of the platform do not properly support resolving `android:onClick`

issue.message: This `<FrameLayout>` can be replaced with a `<merge>` tag
issue.summary: FrameLayout can be replaced with `<merge>` tag
issue.explanation: If a `<FrameLayout>` is the root of a layout and does not provide background or padding etc, it can often be replaced with a `<merge>` tag which is slightly more efficient. Note that this depends on context, so make sure you understand how the `<merge>` tag works before proceeding.

issue.message: The resource `R.layout.candidates` appears to be unused
issue.explanation:
Unused resources make applications larger and slow down builds.
The unused resource check can ignore tests. If you want to include resources that are only referenced from tests, consider packaging them in a test source set instead.
You can include test sources in the unused resource check by setting the system property lint.unused-resources.include-tests =true, and to exclude them (usually for performance reasons), use lint.unused-resources.exclude-tests =true. ,

issue.message: The resource `R.color.candidate_recommended` appears to be unused
issue.explanation:
Unused resources make applications larger and slow down builds.
The unused resource check can ignore tests. If you want to include resources that are only referenced from tests, consider packaging them in a test source set instead.
You can include test sources in the unused resource check by setting the system property lint.unused-resources.include-tests =true, and to exclude them (usually for performance reasons), use lint.unused-resources.exclude-tests =true. ,

issue.message: Launcher icons should not fill every pixel of their square region; see the design guide for details
issue.summary: The launcher icon shape should use a distinct silhouette
issue.explanation:
According to the Android Design Guide (https://material.io/design/iconography/) your launcher icons should "use a distinct silhouette", a "three-dimensional, front view, with a slight perspective as if viewed from above, so that users perceive some depth."
The unique silhouette implies that your launcher icon should not be a filled square.

issue.message: Launcher icons should not fill every pixel of their square region; see the design guide for details
issue.summary: The launcher icon shape should use a distinct silhouette
issue.explanation:
According to the Android Design Guide (https://material.io/design/iconography/) your launcher icons should "use a distinct silhouette", a "three-dimensional, front view, with a slight perspective as if viewed from above, so that users perceive some depth."
The unique silhouette implies that your launcher icon should not be a filled square.

issue.message: Launcher icons should not fill every pixel of their square region; see the design guide for details
issue.summary: The launcher icon shape should use a distinct silhouette
issue.explanation:
According to the Android Design Guide (https://material.io/design/iconography/) your launcher icons should "use a distinct silhouette", a "three-dimensional, front view, with a slight perspective as if viewed from above, so that users perceive some depth."
The unique silhouette implies that your launcher icon should not be a filled square.

issue.message: The `ic_launcher.png` icon has identical contents in the following configuration folders: drawable-mdpi, drawable-xlarge-mdpi
issue.explanation: If an icon is provided under different configuration parameters such as `drawable-hdpi` or `-v11`, they should typically be different. This detector catches cases where the same icon is provided in different configuration folder which is usually not intentional.

issue.message: The `key_bkg.9.png` icon has identical contents in the following configuration folders: drawable-mdpi, drawable-xlarge-mdpi
issue.explanation: If an icon is provided under different configuration parameters such as `drawable-hdpi` or `-v11`, they should typically be different. This detector catches cases where the same icon is provided in different configuration folder which is usually not intentional.

issue.message: The `key_bkg_press.9.png` icon has identical contents in the following configuration folders: drawable-mdpi, drawable-xlarge-mdpi
issue.explanation: If an icon is provided under different configuration parameters such as `drawable-hdpi` or `-v11`, they should typically be different. This detector catches cases where the same icon is provided in different configuration folder which is usually not intentional.

issue.message: The `smiles.png` icon has identical contents in the following configuration folders: drawable-mdpi, drawable-xlarge-mdpi
issue.explanation: If an icon is provided under different configuration parameters such as `drawable-hdpi` or `-v11`, they should typically be different. This detector catches cases where the same icon is provided in different configuration folder which is usually not intentional.

issue.message: The `stroke3_show.png` icon has identical contents in the following configuration folders: drawable-mdpi, drawable-xlarge-mdpi
issue.explanation: If an icon is provided under different configuration parameters such as `drawable-hdpi` or `-v11`, they should typically be different. This detector catches cases where the same icon is provided in different configuration folder which is usually not intentional.

issue.message: Missing the following drawables in `drawable-hdpi`: button.xml, key_bkg.9.png, key_bkg_press.9.png, key_char.png, key_chinese.png... (8 more)
issue.explanation:
Icons will look best if a custom version is provided for each of the major screen density classes (low, medium, high, extra high). This lint check identifies icons which do not have complete coverage across the densities.
Low density is not really used much anymore, so this check ignores the ldpi density. To force lint to include it, set the environment variable `ANDROID_LINT_INCLUDE_LDPI=true`. For more information on current density usage, see https://developer.android.com/about/dashboards

issue.message: Missing density variation folders in `src/main/res`: drawable-xhdpi, drawable-xxhdpi
issue.explanation:
Icons will look best if a custom version is provided for each of the major screen density classes (low, medium, high, extra-high, extra-extra-high). This lint check identifies folders which are missing, such as `drawable-hdpi`.
Low density is not really used much anymore, so this check ignores the ldpi density. To force lint to include it, set the environment variable `ANDROID_LINT_INCLUDE_LDPI=true`. For more information on current density usage, see https://developer.android.com/about/dashboards

issue.message: Custom view `ChangjieKeyboardView` overrides `onTouchEvent` but not `performClick`
issue.explanation: If a `View` that overrides `onTouchEvent` or uses an `OnTouchListener` does not also implement `performClick` and call it when clicks are detected, the `View` may not handle accessibility actions properly. Logic handling the click actions should ideally be placed in `View#performClick` as some accessibility services invoke `performClick` when a click action should occur.

issue.message: 'clickable' attribute found, please also add 'focusable'
issue.explanation: A widget that is declared to be clickable but not declared to be focusable is not accessible via the keyboard. Please add the `focusable` attribute as well.

issue.message: 'clickable' attribute found, please also add 'focusable'
issue.explanation: A widget that is declared to be clickable but not declared to be focusable is not accessible via the keyboard. Please add the `focusable` attribute as well.

issue.message: 'clickable' attribute found, please also add 'focusable'
issue.explanation: A widget that is declared to be clickable but not declared to be focusable is not accessible via the keyboard. Please add the `focusable` attribute as well.

issue.message: 'clickable' attribute found, please also add 'focusable'
issue.explanation: A widget that is declared to be clickable but not declared to be focusable is not accessible via the keyboard. Please add the `focusable` attribute as well.
